title: Specification-First Rust Port: Shared Domain Model + Two Applications
description: Extract and verify the shared domain model (geometry, math, PLC) that both dune_winder and dune_tension depend on. Skeptically audit Python math; treat Python UI behavior as authoritative specification. Build domain-model-first, then two separate applications on top. Zero duplication of specification.

phases.phase: Cross-Reference Allium Specs with Python Implementation
phases.description: The repo has four current Allium specs (winder-states-safety, uv-layer-geometry, head-transfer, tension-measurement; updated Apr 26-27) that are the authoritative domain model. This phase verifies whether Python implementation matches these specs, identifies divergences, and produces decision documents. Allium is the source of truth for the port; Python is an implementation that may lag or require correction.

tasks.title: Verify geometry spec (dune_winder/uv-layer-geometry.allium) against Python implementation
tasks.description: Cross-reference uv-layer-geometry.allium (current spec, Apr 26) entities and rules against src/dune_winder/geometry.py and layout APIs. This is the SHARED foundation for both dune_winder and dune_tension. Check: Are pin ranges correct? Do wire segment formulas match? Does wrap-side classification match Allium rules?
acceptance_criteria: Document produced: docs/audit/geometry-verification.md
Cross-check: UvLayerFrame pin counts, ranges (u_head, u_bottom, u_foot, u_top) per Allium config section
Cross-check: B-side endpoint formulas from Allium guidance against Python segment generation
Cross-check: Opposite-side translation (B↔A) and wrap-side classification logic match Allium rules
Document any deviations: are they bugs in Python, spec errors, or acceptable implementation differences?
Verify: dune_tension shares the same geometry spec (no duplication, both reference one source)

tasks.title: Verify PLC & head-transfer specs (winder-states-safety.allium, head-transfer.allium) against Python
tasks.description: Cross-reference winder-states-safety.allium (state machine, PLC interlocks) and head-transfer.allium (actuator protocol) against src/dune_winder/io/controllers/head.py and PLC communication code. This is SHARED infrastructure both apps depend on. Check: Does Python enforce the PLC state transitions and safety interlocks? Does head transfer implement G206 protocol correctly?
acceptance_criteria: Document produced: docs/audit/plic-and-head-verification.md
Cross-check: PLCController state transitions from Allium match Python PLC state dispatch
Cross-check: ActuatorState position transitions and sensor fusion from head-transfer.allium match Python head.py logic
Cross-check: G206 and G106 transfer protocols from head-transfer.allium match Python implementation
Document timing requirements from Allium (latch_timeout, extend_timeout, sensor settling delays) against Python
Identify any missing invariants or safety rules in Python (e.g., ActuatorEngagementRequiresExtension, MidEngagementIsSafeWithdrawal)

tasks.title: Audit recipe module (dune_winder-specific)
tasks.description: Review src/dune_winder/recipes.py: template variable substitution, validation constraints, error handling. Identify features you dislike or logic that could be simplified. Understand how recipes define winding operations.
acceptance_criteria: Document produced: docs/audit/recipes-review.md with: recipe schema, validation rules catalog, identified design issues
List all validation constraints and their rationale; note which are machine-safety-critical vs. convenience
Identify any ad-hoc string/list handling that should become typed enums in domain model
Note features or behaviors you want to change in dune_winder; distinguish from shared domain model concerns

tasks.title: Audit motion planning (dune_winder-specific)
tasks.description: Review src/dune_winder/motion_plan.py: waypoint generation, motion segment construction, timing equations. Validate against control theory and motion profiles. Understand what freedoms the winding application has in motion planning.
acceptance_criteria: Document produced: docs/audit/motion-review.md with: algorithm explanation, timing model (acceleration/velocity/ramps), edge cases
Verify math against control theory; identify assumptions (max speed, accel limits, interpolation type)
Check: are these timing equations derived from hardware limits or arbitrary? Can they be simplified?
Identify what's truly application-critical vs. optimization

tasks.title: Audit winder state machine and control flow (dune_winder-specific)
tasks.description: Review winder control logic: state transitions, command dispatch, error handling, pause/resume, homing. Identify what operator workflows must be preserved vs. what can be improved.
acceptance_criteria: Document produced: docs/audit/state-machine-review.md with: state diagram, all transitions, edge cases
List all state transitions and identify which are safety-critical vs. convenience
Note implicit assumptions about timing, thread safety, or external event ordering
Distinguish: what behavior must match Python UI (authoritative) vs. what can be corrected

tasks.title: Audit audio/FFT/inference (dune_tension-specific)
tasks.description: Review src/dune_tension/: audio capture, FFT parameters, inference preprocessing, result interpretation. Validate against signal processing best practices. Understand measurement workflow and constraints.
acceptance_criteria: Document produced: docs/audit/tension-review.md with: signal processing chain, FFT setup, inference preprocessing
Verify FFT parameters (window, overlap, normalization) against standard practices; document any non-standard choices
Check inference preprocessing matches model training; identify if model card/metadata exists
Identify: what's measurement domain knowledge vs. implementation detail; what can be optimized

tasks.title: Synthesize domain model specification and architecture decisions
tasks.description: Synthesize all audits into: (1) shared domain model specification (geometry, PLC, shared math), (2) dune_winder application specification, (3) dune_tension application specification. Clarify what's shared vs. application-specific and what can be simplified.
acceptance_criteria: Document produced: docs/audit/DOMAIN_MODEL.md with: shared geometry, coordinate systems, PLC tags/structures, shared equations
Document produced: docs/audit/dune_winder_spec.md with: recipes, motion planning, state machine, preserved operator workflows
Document produced: docs/audit/dune_tension_spec.md with: audio/FFT/inference chain, measurement workflows, preserved operator behaviors
For each component: explicit decision on source of truth (Python math is hypothesis; Python UI/behavior is authoritative)
Identify zero-duplication rules: what must not be copied between applications

tasks.title: Add root Cargo workspace
tasks.description: Create Cargo.toml at root with workspace members pointing to crates/. Add .gitignore rules for Rust build artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: Root Cargo.toml exists with [workspace] and members = ["crates/*"]
cargo check succeeds at root
No conflicts with existing pyproject.toml or Python tooling

tasks.title: Create crates directory skeleton
tasks.description: Create empty directory structure for Rust crates: crates/dune-domain, crates/dune-api-types. No code yet.
acceptance_criteria: Directory structure exists: crates/dune-{domain,api-types}/
Each crate has minimal Cargo.toml with name and version
No compilation errors

tasks.title: Create calibration/ and tests/golden/ directories
tasks.description: Add directory structure for machine/sensor calibration and golden test fixtures. Populate README with ownership rules.
acceptance_criteria: Directories exist: calibration/{machines,sensors,plc}/, tests/golden/
calibration/README.md explains: artifact ownership, format choices (TOML vs JSON), versioning
tests/golden/README.md explains: comparison test purpose, fixture organization

tasks.title: Document Phase 1 completion
tasks.description: Update docs/migration/rust-port-plan.md with rationale, repo structure diagram, and next steps.
acceptance_criteria: File docs/migration/rust-port-plan.md exists
Includes: repository layout diagram, ownership rules, parallel-first strategy rationale
Explains why Python remains at root during port

phases.phase: Specify Domain Model: Shared + Application Types
phases.description: Define the shared domain model (geometry, PLC structures, math constants) used by both dune_winder and dune_tension. Also define application-specific types (recipes, motion, state machine) and external API contracts. This is the single source of truth; no duplication between applications.

tasks.title: Define shared domain model types in Rust
tasks.description: Create crates/dune-domain with: shared geometry definitions (coordinate systems, transforms, machine dimensions), PLC tag structures and queue payloads, mathematical equations and constants. Use serde derive for serialization.
acceptance_criteria: crates/dune-domain/src/lib.rs exports shared types: Geometry, CoordinateSystem, PLC tags/structures, MathConstants, PhysicalLimits
All shared types derive Serialize, Deserialize, Debug, Clone, PartialEq
Geometry module matches docs/audit/DOMAIN_MODEL.md specification (no duplication across apps)
PLC structures and queue payloads match docs/audit/DOMAIN_MODEL.md (single source of truth for ControlLogix interface)
cargo check succeeds; no compiler warnings; ready for both dune_winder and dune_tension to import

tasks.title: Define application-specific types (dune_winder and dune_tension)
tasks.description: Create crates for application-specific types: dune_winder_types (recipes, motion commands, winder state), dune_tension_types (measurement results, audio events). Both depend on crates/dune-domain and avoid duplication. Preserve operator-facing contracts from Python.
acceptance_criteria: crates/dune-winder-types exports: Recipe, WinderCommand, WinderState, MotionSegment, validated per docs/audit/dune_winder_spec.md
crates/dune-tension-types exports: TensionEvent, AudioFrame, SpectrumData, MeasurementResult, validated per docs/audit/dune_tension_spec.md
No duplication of types between dune_winder and dune_tension (geometry, PLC tags, etc. imported from dune-domain)
Operator-facing behavior preserved: same command semantics, same result schema, as Python

tasks.title: Generate TypeScript bindings from Rust domain model
tasks.description: Add ts-rs to Cargo.toml, configure to generate TypeScript from dune-domain, dune_winder_types, dune_tension_types. TypeScript bindings become single source of truth for frontend type contracts.
acceptance_criteria: Cargo.toml includes ts-rs as dev-dependency
Generated TypeScript types cover: Geometry, PLC structures, Recipe, WinderCommand, WinderState, TensionEvent, MeasurementResult
Types check into repo: crates/dune-domain/ts/ or equiv; reviewed as part of domain model changes
Build script auto-generates types on cargo build; CI prevents manual edits to generated files
Frontend developers use generated types directly; no hand-written schema replication

tasks.title: Set up Python↔Rust test harness with audit reference
tasks.description: Create tests/golden/harness.py and tests/golden/harness.rs. Harness serializes Rust types to JSON, imports Python modules, compares outputs. Reference audit findings to identify port-as-is vs. redesigned behaviors.
acceptance_criteria: Rust test: takes Rust struct, serializes to JSON, outputs to file
Python test: reads JSON, deserializes to Python dicts, compares fields
Tests run and pass with: cargo test --test harness, pytest tests/golden/harness.py
Documentation explains: input fixture format, output comparison rules, how to handle redesigned modules
Harness supports two comparison modes: byte-exact (port-as-is) and tolerance-based (redesigned math)

tasks.title: Create golden fixtures from real data with math provenance
tasks.description: Add test fixtures (JSON) for: recipe parsing, geometry calculation, motion plan generation. Validate fixture correctness against docs/audit/DESIGN_DECISIONS.md.
acceptance_criteria: tests/golden/fixtures/ directory with .json files for: recipe_simple.json, geometry_basic.json, motion_plan_example.json
Each fixture includes: inputs, expected_outputs (from Python), math reference (paper, first-principles check, or domain expert validation)
Documented in tests/golden/README.md: fixture purpose, validation method (audit vs. manual verification)
For redesigned modules: include both old (Python) and new (verified) expected outputs

tasks.title: Verify parity for one complete workflow with math validation
tasks.description: Port one deterministic function to Rust (e.g., recipe validation). Run golden tests, confirm Python and Rust outputs match. For redesigned modules, validate new outputs mathematically.
acceptance_criteria: Rust implementation exists in crates/dune-domain or new crates/dune-recipes
If port-as-is: Golden test passes—Python and Rust outputs byte-for-byte identical on fixtures
If redesigned: Rust outputs validated against audit findings; documented why they differ from Python
No regressions in existing Python tests; new Rust implementation ready for next phases

tasks.title: Design review: Geometry module simplifications
tasks.description: Before porting, review docs/audit/geometry-review.md. Decide: port-as-is or apply identified simplifications (e.g., cleaner transforms, better angle handling, simpler boundaries).
acceptance_criteria: Decision documented in crates/dune-geometry/DESIGN.md: port-as-is or list planned simplifications
For each simplification: explain the change, verify it against audit findings, plan how to validate in golden tests
Ready to implement with clear correctness criteria

tasks.title: Port geometry module to Rust with audit-informed design
tasks.description: Implement geometry calculations in crates/dune-geometry: machine bounds, tool offsets, angle wrapping. Apply design decisions from 5.1a.
acceptance_criteria: crates/dune-geometry includes: Geometry struct, machine dimensions, coordinate transforms
Golden tests pass: Rust geometry output matches Python (or validated new outputs if redesigned)
Rust implementation exhaustively checks bounds (no silent overflows or NaNs)
Code documents any deviations from Python with rationale from audit findings

tasks.title: Design review: Recipe validation simplifications
tasks.description: Review docs/audit/recipes-review.md. Decide: port string-based validation as-is, or move to typed enums in Rust? Simplify template substitution logic?
acceptance_criteria: Decision documented in crates/dune-recipes/DESIGN.md: current validation pattern vs. Rust improvements
For each proposed enum/type improvement: validate against existing recipes, plan golden test coverage
Note any breaking changes to recipe format and migration plan

tasks.title: Port recipe validation & expansion to Rust
tasks.description: Move recipe parsing, template variable substitution, and validation from Python to Rust crate. Apply design improvements from 5.2a.
acceptance_criteria: crates/dune-recipes created with Recipe, Step, Template types—using enums instead of strings where audit justified it
Golden tests pass: Rust recipe expansion matches Python dune_winder/recipes.py (or validated improvements)
Rust rejects invalid recipes at compile-time or with exhaustive Result types
All design changes documented with rationale

tasks.title: Design review: Motion planning algorithm improvements
tasks.description: Review docs/audit/motion-review.md. Identify simplifications (e.g., cleaner interpolation, better timing, removed complexity). Plan validation strategy.
acceptance_criteria: Decision documented in crates/dune-motion/DESIGN.md: algorithm to port vs. improvements
If changing timing model: explain new model, compare against old on fixtures, validate numerically
If optimizing waypoint generation: document complexity improvement and correctness verification

tasks.title: Port motion planning to Rust with improvements
tasks.description: Implement waypoint generation, motion segment construction, timing calculation in Rust. Apply audit-informed improvements from 5.3a.
acceptance_criteria: crates/dune-motion created with Waypoint, MotionSegment, PlanResult types
Golden tests pass: Rust motion plans match Python (or documented improvements with validation)
No unsafe math; all divisions checked for zero, all angles bounded
Timing calculations verified against control theory or reference implementations

tasks.title: Design review: Eliminate edge cases from Python state model
tasks.description: Review docs/audit/state-machine-review.md. Identify unsafe transitions, implicit race conditions, or undefined edge cases in Python. Plan Rust enum design to eliminate them.
acceptance_criteria: Document in crates/dune-winder-core/DESIGN.md: identified edge cases from Python, how Rust types prevent them
For each edge case: explicit decision on how Rust will handle it (type system, exhaustive matching, or error variant)
State diagram showing all valid transitions (audit should identify which are missing or unsafe)
Plan how to test that new design prevents previous edge cases

tasks.title: Model exhaustive state machine in Rust enums (audit-informed)
tasks.description: Create crates/dune-winder-core with State enum and StateTransition rules. Encode impossible states as type errors, informed by audit edge cases.
acceptance_criteria: Winder state modeled as exhaustive enum: Idle, Manual, Winding, Paused, Error, Homing, StopRequested
Transitions explicit: from_idle_to_winding(cmd) -> Result<State, TransitionError>
Compiler prevents invalid transitions (e.g., no Resume from Idle) and audit-identified unsafe patterns
All error cases have named variants; errors from audit findings included

tasks.title: Implement state dispatch with exhaustive pattern matching
tasks.description: Add state handler functions that match on state exhaustively. No default arms that hide unhandled cases. Cover audit-identified edge cases.
acceptance_criteria: Command dispatch matches on State exhaustively (compiler error if incomplete)
Each state+command pair has defined behavior or explicit Rejected error
All edge cases from audit are handled: timeouts, cancellations, race conditions, error recovery
Tests explicitly cover audit-identified edge cases

tasks.title: Verify against Python state model with edge case testing
tasks.description: Create golden tests that compare Rust state transitions against Python dune_winder/state_machine.py. Emphasize edge cases identified in audit.
acceptance_criteria: Golden tests exist for: normal winding loop, pause/resume, error recovery, audit-identified edge cases
For port-as-is behaviors: Python and Rust produce identical state sequences
For improved handling (from audit): document why Rust behaves differently and validate against audit rationale
No regressions in existing Python tests; safety improvements from Rust type system evident

tasks.title: Design review: Audio/FFT/inference signal processing
tasks.description: Review docs/audit/tension-review.md. Validate FFT parameters, preprocessing, and inference setup against signal processing best practices. Identify optimizations.
acceptance_criteria: Document in crates/dune-tension-core/DESIGN.md: current FFT setup, validation against best practices
Check: window function type, overlap %, normalization, sampling assumptions
Verify inference preprocessing matches model training (check ONNX model card or metadata)
Identify latency bottlenecks and planned optimizations (vectorization, buffer strategy, etc.)

tasks.title: Create dune-tension-core Rust crate
tasks.description: Set up crate for audio capture, FFT, ONNX inference. Depend on dune-api-types for events.
acceptance_criteria: crates/dune-tension-core exists with dependencies: cpal, realfft, ort, rusqlite, tracing
Core types: AudioFrame, SpectrumData, InferenceResult, TensionEvent
cargo check succeeds; design decisions from 6.0 documented

tasks.title: Implement audio capture and FFT pipeline (audit-verified)
tasks.description: Add audio capture with cpal, FFT with realfft. Apply design decisions from 6.0. Expose TensionEvent that matches Python or improved output.
acceptance_criteria: AudioCapture struct captures from system input with configurable sample rate/format
FFT pipeline uses window/overlap/normalization validated in 6.0
TensionEvent serializes to JSON; format matches Python (or documented improvements)
No latency > 100ms for capture-to-event round-trip; latency monitored and documented

tasks.title: Verify parity with Python tension analysis
tasks.description: Golden tests: compare Rust FFT/inference against Python dune_tension/ on recorded audio samples. Validate math and timing.
acceptance_criteria: Golden test fixtures: recorded audio samples (.wav) with ground-truth annotations
Rust and Python produce identical spectra (within documented floating-point tolerance)
Rust and Python ONNX inference agree on same audio input
Latency comparison: Rust vs. Python on same hardware with measurements logged

tasks.title: Hardware spike: Test rseip against real ControlLogix
tasks.description: Standalone Rust program that connects to actual ControlLogix PLC. Test: tag reads/writes, UDTs, MotionSeg payloads, queue handshakes, reconnects.
acceptance_criteria: Spike code in tools/plc_spike/ directory
Tested against: ordinary tags, UDT structures, MotionSeg-style queue payloads, failure/reconnect scenarios
Decision documented: rseip viable OR proceed with Python pycomm3 sidecar

tasks.title: Choose PLC path based on spike results
tasks.description: If rseip works: create crates/dune-plc-bridge. If not: define Python→Rust IPC boundary for pycomm3 sidecar.
acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded in docs/migration/plc-decision.md with: spike results, chosen path, rationale
If Rust path: crates/dune-plc-bridge skeleton with types from step 8.1
If Python sidecar: IPC protocol documented (Unix socket, gRPC, or HTTP boundary)

phases.phase: Operator UI Contract & One Screen (After Types Proven)

tasks.title: Create SvelteKit project structure
tasks.description: Initialize SvelteKit app in apps/winder-ui with TypeScript, import generated types from crates.
acceptance_criteria: apps/winder-ui/ initialized with SvelteKit, TypeScript
tsconfig points to generated types in src/dune_tension/types/
npm run dev succeeds, Vite dev server starts

tasks.title: Build one non-safety-critical UI screen
tasks.description: Implement status display or recipe preview using generated types, WebSocket connection to existing Python backend.
acceptance_criteria: One SvelteKit page (e.g., /status or /recipe-preview)
Uses generated TypeScript types from dune-api-types
WebSocket or HTTP client connects to Python backend, displays live data
Types prevent common errors (e.g., invalid command variants)

tasks.title: Replace Tkinter with Tauri/Svelte app
tasks.description: After tension core and winder service are proven, consolidate dune_tension GUI into Tauri app.
acceptance_criteria: Tauri app wraps dune-tension-core (Rust) + Svelte UI
Feature parity with current Tkinter: audio capture, spectrum display, inference results, calibration
Single-binary deployment on lab machines

tasks.title: Consolidate winder web UI to SvelteKit
tasks.description: Migrate static HTML winder interface to SvelteKit using proven components and generated types.
acceptance_criteria: Full winder control workflow in SvelteKit
Shares type definitions with tension UI
Proves component reuse and shared design system

decision_points.chosen: Comprehensive audit of geometry, recipes, motion planning, state machine, and audio/FFT/inference
decision_points.rationale: Verify correctness against first principles, identify design issues and simplification opportunities, and document planned changes before porting—ensures Rust port improves on Python foundation

decision_points.chosen: Per-module decisions informed by audit findings. Golden tests support both byte-exact parity (port-as-is) and tolerance-based (redesigned/improved)
decision_points.rationale: Some modules may be correct as-is and benefit from type safety alone; others have edge cases or inefficiencies worth fixing during port. Audit identifies which is which.
Keeps working Python available, enables continuous parity testing against audit-verified baselines, avoids big-bang cutover risk

decision_points.chosen: Type-safety-first with math verification: phases 0, 2, 3, then 5, 6, 7 before UI (phase 4)
decision_points.rationale: Core concern is correctness; audit validates existing math, identifies improvements, type systems enforce safety. Golden tests prevent regressions.
Do not block entire port on unproven Rust PLC library; spike first, choose path after

notes: This plan prioritizes mathematical correctness and design review before porting. Phase 0 audits existing Python code against first principles, identifies edge cases and simplifications, and documents design decisions. Golden parity tests ensure both correctness preservation (for port-as-is modules) and validation of improvements (for redesigned modules). This approach addresses the user's expectation to verify and improve the math during the port, not merely replicate Python in Rust.
